heroHeadline: Pope Leo Declares 'No Fear' After Trump Called Him 'Weak' — Trump-Vatican Rift Sharpens as Pontiff Defies US President Over Iran Peace Appeal
heroSubtitle: President Trump publicly attacked newly elected Pope Leo XIV on April 13, calling him 'weak' and 'terrible for foreign policy' after the Pope appealed for peace in the Iran conflict — triggering immediate diplomatic backlash from European allies and Catholic-majority nations. Separately, Trump acknowledged that gasoline prices may not fall before the November 2026 midterm elections, a significant political admission as the Strait of Hormuz blockade continues to drive energy costs higher. The twin developments come one day after the collapse of the Islamabad talks and Trump's announcement of an immediate US naval blockade of Hormuz.
footerNote: Data sourced from White House, Federal Register, executive orders, court records, Al Jazeera, CNN, Reuters, AP, NPR, Bloomberg, CNBC, Axios, NBC News, CBS News, Gulf News, The National, Newsweek, Nate Silver/Silver Bulletin, YouGov/Economist, Fox News, BLS, Peterson Institute for International Economics, Tax Foundation, CFR, EIA, Treasury, Goldman Sachs, Federal Reserve, OECD, Cato Institute, Penn Wharton Budget Model.
